resources.string: Version, developers, source code and licenses
Required to notify you about proxy and VPN status
Disable optimization so the connection does not sleep in the background
System permission to create a VPN tunnel
If Always-On VPN is enabled, disable it in system VPN settings and grant the permission again
Optional. Required for root features, kernel WireGuard and VPN sharing
Welcome to a unified client for Xray and VK TURN, created for fighting Russian Internet censorship, including whitelists
How do you want to connect?
Let\'s get you back to the Internet freedom!
Fill in the connection parameters or import a ready WINGSV link
No valid VK TURN wingsv:// link found in clipboard
Enter a number greater than zero
Enter endpoint as domain:port or IP:port
Import a VLESS profile or set up basic Xray parameters
No valid Xray wingsv:// link found in clipboard
No valid vless:// link found in clipboard
No valid subscription URL found in clipboard
Configure profile testing parameters before starting
Choose how to test the available profiles
No local Xray profiles yet. Run auto search with Internet at least once so the app can fetch subscriptions, then try again
Xray data is ready. Now connect to a whitelisted network and continue profile testing on it
I connected to a whitelist network
Waiting for captcha lockout to finish: %1$s
No WINGSV or VLESS link in clipboard
Failed to parse wingsv://, vless:// or subscription URL
Add custom DNS in front of the built-in list (Yandex → Google → Cloudflare). One per line.

Formats:
• https://dns.example/dns-query — DoH
• udp://77.88.8.8:53 — plain UDP
• 77.88.8.8 — same, plain UDP/53
Entries that don't respond on bootstrap are dropped; the built-in list remains as fallback.
SRTP-mimicry obfuscation of DTLS bytes inside TURN ChannelData (bypasses VK TURN content filter). Off = transparent, Preferred/Required wrap every datagram.
SRTP-mimicry AEAD. AES-256-GCM uses ARM AES-NI hardware acceleration; ChaCha20-Poly1305 is the software-friendly alternative.
Shared secret (32 bytes = 64 hex chars). Must match the server.
Paste a 64-character hex string (32 bytes). Use the button below to generate a fresh key.
Replaces the current WRAP key with a fresh random 32-byte value.
Send the WRAP key to the server inside the session handshake. Disable if the server already has a fixed key.
Camera is not available on this device
%1$s wants to connect your client to the WINGS V management panel
Switch backend to VK TURN + WireGuard
Permissions from the start screen are required to launch
Error: %1$s\n\nIf you see uplink and downlink traffic and the connection works, you can ignore this error
Select apps that will use a special VPN rule
All apps go through the VPN
Selected apps skip the VPN; everything else is routed through it
Only selected apps go through the VPN; everything else stays direct
Open Xray core access.log and error.log
Open app and service event log
Check whether an update is available
Connecting and checking the latest release…
Latest version %1$s installed. Tap to check again
New release found. Tap to download APK
Install the APK after the download finishes
APK already downloaded. Tap to start installation
Allow installation from unknown sources for WINGS V
Notifications about new WINGS V versions
Open the "About" section to download and install the new version.
APK already downloaded. Open the "About" section to install the update.
Main open-source components of the app
Yeah we never felt so young…
VPNHotspot, librootkotlinx and root tethering architecture
creating One UI and interface design
early One UI Android libraries and UI architecture ideas
One UI modifications and joint SESL work
Xray-core and libXray for Xray backend
ideas and work on VK captcha bypass for vk-turn-proxy
VK traffic-filter bypass via SRTP mimicry
No app found to open the link
Main open-source components used by the app and built-in runtime parts
MIT License • Samsung One UI components
Apache License 2.0 • bundled WireGuard backend for Android
GNU GPL v3 • WINGS-N/vk-turn-proxy (fork of cacggghp/vk-turn-proxy)
Apache License 2.0 • upstream runtime for root tethering
Apache License 2.0 • root server and root command execution
Apache License 2.0 • access to hidden Android APIs in the VPNHotspot runtime
BSD-3-Clause • DNS runtime for the tethering path
Apache License 2.0 • network I/O for the VPN Hotspot DNS runtime
Apache License 2.0 • structured logging in the VPN Hotspot runtime
MIT License • Android bridge and AAR runtime for Xray
Mozilla Public License 2.0 • core runtime for Xray backend
MIT License • front proxy for the Xray backend
Enables the app\'s root capabilities and allows sharing Internet via VPN
For VK TURN + WireGuard, use kernel WireGuard instead of Android VpnService
Transparent traffic interception for Xray via iptables instead of VpnService
Current value: %1$s. {{hex}} is replaced with the hex suffix on launch
Enter a valid Linux interface name up to 15 characters. You may use a single {{hex}} marker
Hide Android VPN signals for selected apps
Spoofs Java Android API VPN signals inside app processes
If disabled, the module only works for the packages listed below
Open the apps list with search
Hides tunnel interfaces in native getifaddrs() and if_nametoindex()
What to return when /proc/net is accessed via native fopen/open. "Filter" mode (recommended) only hides tunnel interface lines and does not break legitimate readers of network tables
What to return to apps that run the system ping to check for ICMP spoofing
Hides tunnel interfaces from system dumpsys reports. Requires a system_server hook
Filters selected packages from PackageManager queries
Open the apps list with search
Native hook applies PLT filtering to the libraries of selected apps. After a change, the app must be restarted. The module must be enabled, and the apps must be selected in Xposed/LSPosed
Settings affect the security level. The fuller the coverage of recommended bypass, Xposed, and local proxy authentication, the higher the level
Too few apps covered by Xposed rules
Not all recommended VPN apps are hidden
ICMP spoofing probe protection works in partial mode
Xray SOCKS password is too weak
ByeDPI SOCKS password is too weak
Not all recommended apps are selected for bypass
Look up VPN services via intents (PackageManager.queryIntentServices())
Read interfaces via native getifaddrs() (native getifaddrs())
Read sysfs interface via fopen() (native fopen(/sys/class/net/<tunnel>/*))
Read sysfs interface via open() (native open(/sys/class/net/<tunnel>/*))
Read sysfs interface via openat() (native openat(/sys/class/net/<tunnel>/*))
Query interface parameters via ioctl() (native SIOCGIF* for tunnel iface)
Retrieve interfaces and routes via NETLINK_ROUTE (native recvmsg() for RTM_NEWLINK / RTM_NEWADDR / RTM_NEWROUTE)
Retrieve NETLINK_ROUTE data via recvmsg() (native recvmsg() on tracked route socket)
Retrieve NETLINK_ROUTE data via recv() (native recv() on tracked route socket)
Retrieve NETLINK_ROUTE data via recvfrom() (native recvfrom() on tracked route socket)
Retrieve NETLINK_ROUTE data via recvmmsg() (native recvmmsg() on tracked route socket)
Retrieve NETLINK_ROUTE data via read() (native read() on tracked route socket)
Enumerate sockets via NETLINK_SOCK_DIAG (native SOCK_DIAG_BY_FAMILY with tunnel idiag_if)
Filter tunnel interface lines in /proc/net/* (native read on tracked procfs fd)
Probe tunnel interface existence via access()/faccessat() (native access /sys/class/net/<tunnel> or /dev/tun)
Read tunnel interface metadata via stat()/lstat()/fstatat()/statx()/statfs()
Spoof system ping result during an ICMP spoofing probe
WB Stream backend is temporarily unavailable
RAW config or set up via UI
Paste an awg-quick config from the clipboard and split it into fields
Structured fields edit one Interface and the first Peer. For multi-peer configs, use RAW config
Failed to parse awg-quick config from clipboard
Quick switch of the active backend
List of subscription URLs, refresh and sync interval
DNS, IPv6, local proxy and network parameters
LiveKit room, VK TURN exchange, WireGuard/AmneziaWG parameters
If empty, the client will create a new room and show the ID
Exchange room data via VK TURN
Send room ID to the server via a short VK TURN handshake instead of exchanging it manually
How many LiveKit rooms to open in parallel (1–16). With >1, traffic is distributed round-robin across rooms to bypass SFU per-room limits
Additionally encrypt packets with AES-256-GCM on top of the LiveKit DataChannel
Pre-shared secret in Base64. Must match on client and server
Endpoint, VK Link and parameters for Xray over VK TURN TCP
VPN starts the system VPN; proxy-only starts a local proxy without a VPN Service
Direct site checks bypassing VPN, with automatic backend switching
When Xray, WireGuard or AmneziaWG is active, checks sites in the background outside the VPN and, if all checks fail, switches the backend to a TURN variant
Available only on Xray, WireGuard and AmneziaWG backends
Where to switch from Xray when direct checks fail
When a TURN backend is active, checks the same sites outside the VPN and switches the backend back to the original direct variant when checks pass again
When the tunnel is down, checks sites in the background outside the VPN and automatically brings up the selected fallback backend if all checks fail
Manage the list of sites for direct checks
These sites are checked directly, bypassing the VPN. If all sites are unreachable, auto-switch triggers
A valid http:// or https:// URL is required
Site %1$s will be removed from the check list
Notifications about automatic backend switching after direct checks
Sites %1$s did not respond outside the VPN. Switching from %2$s to %3$s
Background auto-switch enabled the fallback backend
Sites %1$s did not respond outside the VPN. Starting %2$s automatically
Sites %1$s are reachable again outside the VPN. Switching from %2$s to %3$s
Select the active profile and manage subscriptions
Open subscription URL management and refresh
Choose a test type for profiles in the "%1$s" filter
Check TCP availability for all profiles in the current filter
Check TCP availability for all profiles in the "%1$s" filter
No profiles with an address and port in the current filter
No profiles with a source link in the current filter
Select an action for the checked profiles
Export in wingsv:// or vless:// format
Failed to build a link for the selected profiles
Reset uplink and downlink for the selected profiles
Delete the selected profiles from the list
Add subscription URLs and refresh the profile list manually or on a schedule
Refresh profiles from all saved subscriptions
Subscription saved, but no profiles were found
Failed to build a link for the selected subscriptions
Send x-hwid, x-device-os, x-ver-os and x-device-model when refreshing subscriptions
Use manually entered values instead of automatic ones
Automatic HWID and device data transmission when refreshing subscriptions
Manual HWID and device data values when refreshing subscriptions
Opens the local proxy to other devices on the local network
Allows insecure TLS where the profile supports it
Use IPv6 in the Xray runtime
Destination detection via traffic sniffing for transparent mode

Grace window for resolving the UID of a new TCP/UDP connection via /proc/net before the per-app filter drops it. Helps Tor/SSH that open many sockets per second by giving the kernel time to publish them. 0 disables the retry. Recommended: 50
Closes leaks from apps that bind tun0 directly.
Allow UDP/443 to be sent through Xray. If disabled, QUIC is blocked and apps fall back to TCP
Restart Xray when network interface changes
Re-establish Xray when switching between Wi-Fi, mobile data and other physical networks
How Xray delivers the TCP connection to the remote server
Set up geoip/geosite files and custom routing rules
Start a local SOCKS inbound for Xray
Require username and password for the local SOCKS proxy
Start a local HTTP inbound for Xray
Require username and password for the local HTTP proxy
What to do on screen-on / unlock to verify tunnel liveness
Are you sure you want to continue?
You are disabling SOCKS server authentication. This reduces the security of your connection
The SOCKS server password is too simple. This reduces the security of your connection
You are disabling HTTP proxy authentication. This reduces the security of your connection

You are disabling the leak guard for unknown-UID packets. Apps that bind tun0 directly (e.g. via SO_BINDTODEVICE or explicit source IP) will be tunneled through the VPN even when they are in the bypass list, deanonymizing them.
The HTTP proxy password is too simple. This reduces the security of your connection
You are disabling TLS certificate verification for Xray outbounds. This makes the connection vulnerable to MITM attacks
These files are used for geoip/geosite routing rules
Rule order matters: the first match wins before the generic proxy rule
Add, reorder and validate routing rules
The more links, the more stable the connection and the less captcha. Order sets priority: top links are tried first; on failure, the client switches to the next.
Sign in with VK and create a call automatically
VK API client id is not set in this build
The list is empty. WINGS V can sign you into VK and create a call link automatically. Continue?
After signing in, WINGS V will create one VK call and add its link to your list.
VK API client id is not configured in this build
WebView is unavailable on this device
Not set — fallback is not used
Used when all primary links fail
No links. Add at least one to start the tunnel.
If this is the last link, the tunnel will not be able to obtain TURN credentials. Continue?
How many workers share one VK credential. Fewer — more credentials (higher rate-limit ceiling, but more captcha requests). Default is 12.
Do not try to solve VK captcha automatically and wait for manual confirmation right away
Algorithm for automatically passing VK captcha
Restart VK TURN when network interface changes
Re-establish VK TURN when switching between Wi-Fi, mobile data and other physical networks
Confirm you are not a robot

The check is required to bring up the tunnel via VK. Complete the actions in the window below to continue. After passing the captcha, the connection may take a moment, please wait and do not reconnect early. If the captcha is requested several more times, complete it again and again until it stops being requested.

The check is required for background refresh of an additional TURN session. Complete the actions in the window below to keep the connection stable. If you press "Cancel", the currently active connection will not be disconnected.
Waiting for the check to complete
Failed to load the check page
Background refresh of TURN connections is waiting for a captcha. Tap to open the one-time check window
An additional VK TURN session requires a captcha. Tap to open the check window
Follow the device light or dark theme
Always use the dark app appearance
Always use the light app appearance
Affects VK TURN and WB Stream
Remote settings and tunnel management via panel
The owner of the panel will be able to fully manage this WINGS V client: change any settings, view all logs, and enable or disable the connection. Continue?
After unlinking, the panel will lose control of this device. Continue?
Connect to the panel right after boot
Removes the token from this device
Automatically bring up the connection after the system boots
VPN sharing lets you forward the VPN connection to other devices without installing any third-party software on them
Enable root features in settings to route system tethering through the VPN
Allow changing system settings to manage system tethering on this device
Open the system screen and allow changing system settings for WINGS V
Routing changes take effect after reconnect or via "Re-apply routing rules"
Weekly traffic chart and connected clients
No traffic recorded yet. Start sharing to populate.
Wi-Fi hotspot parameters for VPN sharing
USB tethering parameters for clients connected over USB
Wi-Fi lock, offload, temporary hotspot configuration and safe mode
DHCP workaround and settings affecting USB clients
The system did not confirm the sharing state change
Refresh active routing rules without a full reconnect
Bring up the VPN connection first
Reduces the risk of IPv6 leaks outside the VPN
After the system boots, automatically restores the last active sharing endpoints on the selected backend
Bumps TTL/HL of tether traffic by +1 via iptables so carrier-side detection sees direct mobile packets instead of forwarded ones. Requires root and the xt_HL kernel module.
Disable if system tethering does not route traffic through the VPN
Enable if clients do not get IP addresses
Kept for compatibility with repeater modes
Use system configuration for temporary hotspot
May conflict with other apps using a local-only hotspot
When Xray starts, ByeDPI will automatically attach to it as a proxy
Switches ByeDPI to direct arguments mode instead of UI settings
Require username and password for the ByeDPI SOCKS proxy
A higher value improves accuracy but may reduce stability
A higher value speeds up the test but may reduce stability
A higher value slows down the test but improves stability and accuracy
Test strategies via the local ByeDPI proxy and apply the best one manually
Tests prepared strategies through the local ByeDPI proxy and shows the most effective ones
Best strategy: %1$d out of %2$d successful requests
These domain lists are used during automatic ByeDPI strategy tuning
A list with this name already exists
Copy a wingsv:// link with the selected settings
All proto app settings, subscriptions and routing without profiles from subscriptions
Manually added profiles, subscriptions, Xray parameters and routing rules
Only custom Xray routing rules and geo file links
Endpoint, VK Link, session mode and vk-turn-proxy parameters
Endpoint, Interface and Peer for WireGuard
Raw AmneziaWG configuration and the selected AmneziaWG backend
Room ID, display name, E2E key; when exchange via VK TURN is enabled — also VK TURN parameters
Module toggles, hook modes, lists of target and hidden VPN apps
Only the list of apps selected for VPN bypass
Stops the current tunnel, refreshes subscriptions, tests profiles without a VPN connection, and offers to apply the best channel found
Temporarily disables the current tunnel and tests available Xray profiles
Choose start to refresh subscriptions and pick a stable profile automatically
Preparing automatic search and saving the current state
Checking connectivity without the tunnel and refreshing subscription profiles
Bringing up the ByeDPI proxy to test profiles on a WL network
Checking that HTTP traffic passes through the profile
Testing profiles by downloading a test file…
Stable profile found: %1$s. Apply the found configuration?
Enable ByeDPI and test profiles with it
Failed to fetch profiles for testing
Failed to stop the current tunnel
Failed to start ByeDPI for auto search
Failed to wait for the Xray proxy
WebView is unavailable, captcha will open in the external browser
available only on the Xray backend
Available only for the WireGuard backend
Kernel WireGuard is not available on this device
Kernel module xt_TPROXY is not loaded
GitHub did not return a published release
No APK asset found in the release
Failed to locate the installed APK
Failed to replace the old APK
Local endpoint is not filled in
VK Link is not filled in
Local endpoint is not filled in
For proxy-only mode enable the local Xray SOCKS proxy
AmneziaWG config is not filled in
WireGuard private key is not filled in
WireGuard addresses are not filled in
WireGuard public key is not filled in
WireGuard allowed IPs are not filled in
Failed to derive VLESS link from JSON profile
Failed to derive outbound from profile
Port must be 1-65535 or a range
Failed to fully clean up the previous backend before reconnect
Previous VPN backend has not transitioned to DOWN yet; starting Xray is not possible
. Check another active VPN and Always-On VPN in the system settings
Failed to start the Xray VPN service
Failed to open the Xray TUN
Failed to start the Xray protect bridge
Xray core did not transition to the running state
Userspace VPN backend has not transitioned to DOWN yet; starting proxy-only is not possible
ByeDPI protect socket is not initialized
ByeDPI must start only with protect(fd)
ByeDPI did not open the local proxy in time
Local endpoint must be in host:port format
Local endpoint must contain a host
Local endpoint contains an invalid port
vk-turn-proxy exited before opening the local TCP relay
vk-turn-proxy did not open the local TCP relay in time
Xray VPN service has not transitioned to DOWN yet; starting userspace WireGuard is not possible
Root runtime is no longer active
VK TURN handshake for room id exchange failed: %1$s
VK TURN endpoint points at localhost (%1$s). Room exchange goes to the VK TURN 3x-ui server; set its public endpoint in VK TURN settings and keep 127.0.0.1:9000 only in \"Local endpoint\".
vk-turn-proxy did not reach the ready state
vk-turn-proxy passed auth but did not reach TURN/DTLS ready state
Failed to start VPN protect bridge
Failed to start AmneziaWG protect bridge
Previous VPN backend has not transitioned to DOWN yet; starting protect bridge is not possible
Failed to determine the upstream route for root mode: default route not found
Root tether routing skipped: live root tunnel not found
Failed to create the TPROXY config directory
Failed to restrict permissions on the TPROXY config
Xray TPROXY listener did not open on 0.0.0.0:%1$d in time
Xray TPROXY runtime started under root, listener on :%1$d
Root app tunnel routing skipped: table for %1$s not found
Root tether NAT fallback: iptables nat unavailable, switching to netd
Userspace VPN backend has not transitioned to DOWN yet; starting Xray is not possible
Previous userspace VPN backend has not transitioned to DOWN yet; start is not possible
Previous VPN backend has not transitioned to DOWN yet; starting %1$s is not possible
VPN permission is unavailable. Check the system VPN permission, another active VPN and Always-On VPN
VPN permission became unavailable while starting Xray. Check the system VPN permission, another active VPN and Always-On VPN
Failed to open Xray TUN. Possible conflict with another VPN, Always-On VPN, or device firmware
Failed to open Xray TUN: system VPN permission is unavailable. Check another VPN and Always-On VPN
Failed to open Xray TUN. Possible conflict with another VPN, Always-On VPN, or firmware restrictions
Failed to apply app routing for Xray
libXray returned an empty outbound config
Failed to derive outbound from JSON profile
Failed to derive outbound from share-link profile
Xray outbound override cannot be applied
Current Xray outbound does not support VK TURN TCP relay
Failed to derive outbound from VLESS profile
Failed to extract VLESS link from JSON profile
Failed to extract outbound from profile
